title: District Advisory Board
rationale: Purely a district-level advisory/communications governance body with no decisions about an individual student or staff member and no data, technology, or monitoring provisions.

title: Nondiscrimination/Equal Opportunity
rationale: Governs consequential discrimination/harassment determinations affecting students' education opportunity and staff employment, with annual notice, investigation records, and an appeal/human-review structure.

title: Harassment and Discrimination Investigation Procedures for Students
rationale: Implements an investigation, written-report, and appeal process that produces consequential discipline/education-opportunity decisions about individual students, with recordkeeping obligations.

title: Harassment and Discrimination Investigation Procedures for Employees
rationale: Establishes investigation and disciplinary decision-making about individual employees' employment status, with confidential personnel records subject to retention rules.

title: Sexual Harassment Investigation Procedures
rationale: Defines a decision-maker-driven Title IX process resulting in consequential disciplinary determinations about students/staff, with a formal appeal and written-record requirements.

title: Nondiscrimination on the Basis of Disability
rationale: Governs disability eligibility/placement determinations and a multi-step grievance and due-process hearing (human review/appeal) affecting students' education opportunity and staff employment.

title: Tobacco-Free and Marijuana-Free District
rationale: States a conduct prohibition with generic disciplinary consequences but establishes no decision process, records system, or data/technology practice of its own (procedures live in cross-referenced policy JICH).

title: Student and Employee Wellness
rationale: Programmatic wellness-policy governance (advisory council, nutrition/PE standards) with no individualized decision, records, or technology component.

title: Student and Employee Wellness (Regulation)
rationale: Implementation detail for wellness programming and food/PE standards; no consequential decision about an individual and no data/tech handling.

title: Communicable Diseases
rationale: Authorizes exclusion of individual students/employees from attendance and expressly governs disclosure/confidentiality of health data under FERPA/HIPAA/CORA.

title: Animals in District Facilities and Vehicles
rationale: The Section 504/ADA Compliance Officer makes individualized service-animal accommodation and exclusion determinations affecting a disabled student's or employee's access to district programs.

title: District and School Accountability
rationale: Establishes advisory-committee governance and budget/plan review functions with no decision about a specific individual and no data/tech handling.

title: District Governance
rationale: Purely internal governance/administrative-authority framework with no individual consequential decision or data/tech provisions.

title: School Building Administration
rationale: General statement of principal administrative authority; no specific decision process about an individual student/staff member and no data/tech content.

title: Site-based School Management
rationale: Allocates discretionary management authority to principals at the school-operations level; not a decision about an individual and no data/tech handling.

title: Procedures for Superintendent Policies
rationale: Purely a policy-drafting/adoption workflow with no individual decision or data/technology component.

title: Research Involving District Students, Employees or Resources
rationale: Directly governs analysis of student/employee personally identifiable education-record data, requires informed parental/student consent, contracting with third-party researchers, and recordkeeping/termination authority.

title: Relations with Other Schools and School Systems
rationale: High-level intergovernmental cooperation framework; references data exchange only in the abstract with no specific personal-data, decision, or technology process.

title: Charter Schools
rationale: Governs entity-level charter application, renewal, and accountability review of schools, not a consequential decision about an identifiable individual student or staff member.

title: Construction Projects and Contracting Procedures
rationale: Purely operational/facilities contracting procedure with no decision about a person and no data/tech handling.

title: Construction Project Prequalification
rationale: Vendor/contractor prequalification using human-applied predetermined criteria, not a decision about a student or staff member and not a data/tech process regulated by the Act.

title: Naming District Sites and Facilities
rationale: Purely administrative facility-naming procedure with no decision about a specific individual's rights/eligibility and no data/tech handling.

title: School Closure, Consolidation, and/or Relocation (source fetch failed)
rationale: Title and cross-referenced FCB-R content confirm this governs Board decisions on school closure/consolidation/relocation that materially affect students' enrollment and education opportunity via a superintendent-recommendation/Board-decision human-review process; content unavailable so flagged for manual confirmation.

title: School Closure Implementation Processes (source fetch failed)
rationale: Title and parallel full-text version confirm this sets data-driven criteria (enrollment/utilization projections) feeding a human decision-making process on school closures affecting student education opportunity; content unavailable so flagged for manual confirmation.

title: Student Attendance/Truancy
rationale: Establishes an explicit absence-monitoring/tracking system, individualized truancy determinations affecting a student's education opportunity, and a defined appeal path to the assistant superintendent and superintendent.

title: Compliance with the Family and Medical Leave Act
rationale: Governs individualized FMLA eligibility/leave-designation decisions about employees, including collection of medical certification records and district notification timelines.

title: Public Concerns/Complaints About Personnel
rationale: Establishes a complaint/investigation/resolution process about individual staff members with records cross-referenced to the personnel-records policy, which can lead to further personnel action.

title: Public Concerns/Complaints About Teaching (Including Methods, Activities, or Presentations)
rationale: Multi-step review-committee and appeal process regarding a teacher's methods/materials, with outcomes documented in the employee's personnel file, engaging human-review and recordkeeping duties.

title: Distribution/Posting of Non-District Communications
rationale: Purely operational rules for posting/distributing third-party advertising and notices; no decision about an individual and no personal data/technology handling.

title: Relations with State and Federal Agencies to Enhance the Safety and Security of Students, Employees, and the Community
rationale: Directly regulates collection, disclosure, and confidentiality of student education records (FERPA) and immigration/citizenship data, including a designated records point of contact.

title: Annual Budget
rationale: Purely fiscal budget-format and preparation policy with no vendor-technology, personal-data, records-access, or person-decision content.

title: General Fund Balances
rationale: Governs GASB fund-balance categories and reserve levels, a purely fiscal accounting matter unrelated to ADMT, vendors, or records.

title: Federal Procurement and Fiscal Requirements
rationale: Establishes procurement thresholds, conflict-of-interest rules, and mandatory procurement-record retention for federally funded purchases of services/equipment, which may include technology, implicating vendor-binding and recordkeeping duties.

title: Incentive Payments to Taxpayers
rationale: Governs tax-incentive negotiations with local businesses, unrelated to technology vendors, records access, or person-level decisions.

title: Incentive Payments to Taxpayers (Regulation)
rationale: Implements the tax-incentive negotiation process with local governments; no vendor-technology, data, or consequential-decision content.

title: Revenues from Investment/Use of Surplus Funds
rationale: Governs investment of District funds and broker/dealer standards, a purely fiscal treasury matter outside ADMT scope.

title: Free Admissions
rationale: Establishes lifetime activity pass eligibility categories, a purely administrative/logistics matter with no vendor, data, or ADMT content.

title: Authorized Signatures on District Checks
rationale: Sets check-signing authorization rules, a purely fiscal/logistics matter unrelated to technology vendors or ADMT.

title: Employee Surety Bonds/Insurance
rationale: Requires bonding/insurance for employees with custody of funds, a purely fiscal risk-management matter with no ADMT relevance.

title: Audits/Financial Monitoring
rationale: Governs annual financial-statement audits and auditor qualifications, a purely fiscal/audit matter with no vendor-technology or person-decision content.

title: Audit Committee
rationale: Establishes the District Audit Committee's fiscal-oversight role and membership, unrelated to technology vendors, records access, or ADMT decisions.

title: Internal Audit Services
rationale: Defines internal-auditor scope limited to financial/compliance review functions, with no vendor-technology or ADMT-relevant content.

title: Purchasing
rationale: Governs procurement of goods and services (including any technology/software), vendor selection criteria, and directs purchase-record requests through CORA, directly implicating vendor-binding and recordkeeping duties.

title: Petty Cash
rationale: Governs small-dollar cash transactions and custodian bookkeeping, a purely fiscal/logistics matter with no vendor-technology or data content.

title: School Properties Disposition
rationale: Governs disposal/reuse of surplus property including obsolete technology equipment, but concerns end-of-life asset logistics rather than vendor procurement, data handling, or person-affecting decisions.

title: Vendor Relations, Sales Calls and Demonstrations
rationale: Directly governs vendor qualification/prequalification, conflict-of-interest standards, and vendor disqualification, core to SB 26-189's vendor/developer-binding duty.

title: Expense Authorization/Reimbursement
rationale: Governs employee mileage/travel reimbursement, a purely fiscal/logistics matter unrelated to vendors, data, or ADMT.

title: Purchasing Authority, Methods, and Thresholds
rationale: Establishes competitive bidding/proposal methods, sole-source and standardization exceptions, and vendor grievance/appeal procedures central to vendor procurement governance.

title: Contracting
rationale: Governs all District contracts including software purchases via standard templates, requires legal-counsel review, and expressly cross-references the Student Data Transparency and Security Act and Colorado Privacy Act, making it the primary anchor for vendor/developer binding and data-protection contract terms.

title: Financial Reports and Statements
rationale: Governs monthly reconciliations and budget reporting, a purely fiscal matter with no vendor, data-privacy, or ADMT content.

title: Title I Parent Involvement
rationale: Governs parent engagement in Title I program planning and communication, with no vendor-technology, records-access, or ADMT decision content.

title: Support Organizations
rationale: Governs independent booster clubs/PTAs and their gift/fund handling, an organizational-relations matter with no ADMT, vendor-technology, or public-records content.

title: Public Information and Communications
rationale: High-level statement of the Board's communications philosophy with no complaint procedure, records process, or vendor-technology content.

title: Public Inspection and Copying of District Records
rationale: Implements CORA public-records inspection, fee, exemption, and denial procedures directly applicable to public access/handling of District records and data, a core SB 26-189 recordkeeping touchpoint.

title: Crisis Management and Prevention
rationale: Governs emergency-response planning under NIMS/ICS; its only 'automated' reference is AED medical-device hardware, not an algorithmic/ADMT decision system.

title: Public Concerns/Complaints about Instructional Resources
rationale: Establishes a reconsideration/appeal process for challenged instructional materials, a curriculum-content complaint procedure rather than one reviewing an automated consequential decision about a person.

title: Public Concerns/Complaints Form
rationale: Standardized intake form implementing KEC's instructional-materials complaint process; no ADMT, vendor-technology, or data-correction content.

title: Public Concerns/Complaints about Teaching (form)
rationale: A referral notice directing complainants to central administration for a teaching-methods challenge form; no vendor-technology, records, or ADMT content.

title: Use of District Facilities
rationale: Governs facility scheduling, fees, and denial appeals for building/field use, a facilities-logistics matter with no vendor-technology or ADMT content.

title: Public Conduct on District Property
rationale: Sets behavioral standards and trespass enforcement on District property, unrelated to automated systems, vendors, or records.

title: District Revenue through Advertising
rationale: Governs advertising/sponsorship approval and revenue allocation; involves vendor agreements but not technology/software procurement, personal data, or person-level decisions.

title: Visitors to Schools
rationale: Governs visitor check-in, identification, and classroom-observation limits through manual procedures; explicitly no automated screening system, vendor technology, or scoring tool is described.

title: Volunteers
rationale: Requires a District-engaged background-check vendor, retains confidential screening records, and provides a denial/appeal process to the executive director of HR, making it a natural anchor for vendor-binding and human-review duties if any screening tool becomes ADMT.

title: Volunteers (Regulation)
rationale: Operationalizes volunteer screening through a background-check contractor using national criminal databases with defined escalation criteria and confidential-file recordkeeping, warranting inventory/vendor review even though no automated scoring tool is currently described.

title: Relations with Election Authorities
rationale: Authorizes principals as deputy voter registrars, a narrow election-administration matter with no vendor-technology or ADMT content.

title: Relations with Local Governmental Authorities
rationale: Establishes general information-sharing commitments with local government, unrelated to technology vendors, records access, or ADMT decisions.

title: Hazardous Substances
rationale: Purely a chemical/hazardous-materials safety and disposal policy with no decision about a person and no data, technology, records, or monitoring subject matter.

title: Prevention of Disease/Infection Transmission
rationale: Body text governs accident reporting procedures for insurance/litigation purposes with no automated decision, eligibility determination, or technology/data-processing content.

title: Prevention of Disease/Infection Transmission
rationale: Purely clinical/procedural infection-control guidance (universal precautions, hand washing, PPE) with no decision about a person and no data or technology handling.

title: Fire and Emergency Evacuation Response Drills
rationale: Purely operational fire-drill scheduling and reporting requirements; no consequential decision about an individual and no data/technology component.

title: School Closings and Cancellations
rationale: Operational weather-closure decision-making by the superintendent based on human assessment of road/weather data; not a consequential decision about a specific person and no ADMT, data, or records subject matter.

title: Property Management
rationale: General facilities/property oversight policy; mentions transportation and food services only as administrative areas without setting eligibility criteria or touching data/technology.

title: Securing District Facilities
rationale: Physical key/access-card security policy; issuance is tied to employment/contract status but involves no personal-data processing, monitoring technology, or consequential decision-making system.

title: Vandalism
rationale: Student discipline for vandalism is handled through direct human fact-finding and reporting with no data processing, technology, or automated system involved.

title: Electronic Monitoring and Surveillance
rationale: Governs camera/telephone monitoring technology, notice, retention, and access-logging of recordings capturing students and staff, squarely within SB 26-189's monitoring/records/notice concerns even though no AI analytics are currently disclosed.

title: Energy Conservation
rationale: Purely facilities/energy-efficiency policy with no decision about a person and no data or technology handling.

title: Waste Management and Recycling
rationale: Purely operational waste-reduction and recycling policy with no decision about a person and no data or technology handling.

title: District Transportation Services and Vehicle Use
rationale: Operational bus-use prioritization and driver authorization logistics with no data/technology component and no automated eligibility or benefit determination.

title: Transportation Students To and From School
rationale: Transportation eligibility is a manual, rule-based distance measurement administered by district staff with no automated system, data processing, or essential-benefit determination as defined under the Act.

title: Drug and Alcohol Testing for Drivers with CDL
rationale: Federally mandated lab-based drug/alcohol testing program producing objective chemical results reviewed by humans; no automated decision-making technology, profiling, or scoring of the individual is involved.

title: Drug and Alcohol Testing for District CDL Drivers
rationale: Implementing regulation for the same lab-based federal testing program; consequences flow from confirmed lab results and human/supervisor determinations, not ADMT.

title: Student Conduct on School Buses
rationale: Bus-riding privilege and discipline policy administered entirely by human staff with no data/technology or automated-decision content.

title: Student Conduct on School Buses
rationale: Operational conduct, progressive-discipline, and bus-stop-assignment logistics for already-eligible students, with no automated system or data/technology handling.

title: Use of Buses by Non-Student Groups
rationale: Purely operational bus-rental scheduling, fees, and application logistics for community groups with no data/technology handling and no student/staff consequential decision.

title: Student Transportation in Private Vehicles
rationale: Driver/vehicle approval criteria for field-trip transportation are manually administered eligibility checks with no data/technology or automated-decision component.

title: Food Services
rationale: General cafeteria operations and nutrition-program oversight policy; the substantive free/reduced-price eligibility determination is delegated entirely to companion policy EFC.

title: Free and Reduced Price Food Services
rationale: Establishes the district's essential-benefit eligibility determination (free/reduced-price meals) with an application, income-verification, and appeals process squarely within SB 26-189's covered domains.

title: Copyright Compliance
rationale: Copyright-law compliance for reproduction of materials; no decision about a person and no personal-data, monitoring, or automated-system content.

title: District Information Technology
rationale: Governs acquisition/approval of District technology, student/employee/volunteer data handling and downloading restrictions, and monitoring of technology use, making it the natural locus for an ADMT inventory and vendor-approval process.

title: Records Retention
rationale: Establishes the district's general and litigation-hold records-retention framework that would govern retention of ADMT-related records and logs required by SB 26-189.

title: Compliance with HIPAA
rationale: Governs handling of protected health information and delegates development of data-handling safeguards and vendor compliance activities to a privacy official, implicating personal-data domains SB 26-189 regulates.

title: Harassment or Discrimination of Employees
rationale: Purely a conduct-standard and complaint-investigation policy enforced through human judgment, with no personnel-data system, technology monitoring, or ADMT-relevant decision process described.

title: Employee Conduct and Ethics, Conflict of Interest, and Familial and Personal Relationships
rationale: Contains fingerprint-based CBI/FBI criminal-history screening of candidates and current employees that directly drives hiring rescission, discipline, and dismissal decisions.

title: Staff Dress Code
rationale: Purely administrative appearance standard with no personnel decision-making process, data handling, or technology component.

title: Employee Communications
rationale: General conduct standard for official and off-duty communications enforced through human-judgment discipline, with no data system, monitoring technology, or automated evaluation described.

title: Employee Use and Possession of Drugs and Alcohol
rationale: Conduct prohibition with human-administered disciplinary consequences; no automated screening, scoring, or data/technology governance is described.

title: Acknowledgment and Agreements Regarding Employee Use and Possession of Drugs and Alcohol
rationale: A signature/acknowledgment exhibit form with no decision-making process or data/technology handling of its own.

title: Employee Use of District Information Technology
rationale: Governs District technology use, monitoring without notice, and handling of confidential personnel and student data across District and third-party systems, squarely within SB 26-189's technology-use/monitoring scope.

title: Violence in the Workplace
rationale: Incident-reporting and investigation policy with human-judgment discipline; no personnel-data system or automated tool is described.

title: Staff Health
rationale: Governs pre-employment medical screening that conditions job offers and fitness-for-duty evaluations that can lead to suspension or termination, involving confidential health-data handling.

title: Staff Health (Regulation)
rationale: Details the fitness-for-duty and communicable-disease determination process that can result in suspension, leave, or dismissal, based on confidential medical review data.

title: Staff Personal Security and Safety
rationale: Addresses student misconduct toward staff and resulting student discipline, not a consequential employment decision about staff nor any data/technology system.

title: Compliance with Family and Medical Leave Act
rationale: Directly governs leave determinations for staff (eligibility, reinstatement, key-employee denial), a covered employment decision category under the Act.

title: Fair Campaign Practices Compliance
rationale: An ethics/compliance restriction on use of District resources for campaign activity, with no decision about an individual employee's status and no data/tech system.

title: Personnel Records and Files
rationale: Directly governs classification, confidentiality, access, and disclosure of personnel records and evaluation data, core recordkeeping/classification territory under the Act.

title: Criminal History Record Information
rationale: Governs storage, access, and account-management controls for FBI/CBI criminal justice information used in criminal-history determinations about staff.

title: Displaced Teachers
rationale: Ties consequential decisions (reassignment denial, reduction-in-force, priority-pool eligibility) to performance evaluation ratings, a covered employment-decision category.

title: Resignation of Instructional Staff/Administrative Staff
rationale: Governs resignation processing and mandatory reporting of misconduct-related resignations to CDE, involving personnel record handling tied to a staff separation event.

title: Resignation of Classified Staff
rationale: Governs resignation notice and mandatory CDE reporting of child-related misconduct allegations, involving personnel record handling tied to a staff separation event.

title: Classified Employee Performance Trial Periods, Discipline and Dismissal
rationale: The core policy governing classified-staff performance trial outcomes, discipline, and dismissal, squarely within the Act's covered employment-decision categories.

title: Choice Enrollment of Children of District Employees
rationale: Governs student enrollment eligibility for employees' children, not a consequential employment decision about staff and no personnel data/technology system.

title: School Year/Calendar/Instructional Time
rationale: Sets calendar length and defines instructional time district-wide; contains no decision about an individual student or staff member.

title: Curriculum Development
rationale: Governs cooperative curriculum development process, not an individualized decision about a student or staff member.

title: Curriculum Adoption
rationale: Establishes Board approval process for new programs/courses at the program level, not a decision about any individual person.

title: Student Activities and Organizations
rationale: Governs discretionary approval and operation of student clubs/organizations; not a listed consequential decision (admission, placement, grading, graduation, or formal program eligibility).

title: Curriculum Review
rationale: Directs periodic aggregate curriculum evaluation, not an individualized student decision.

title: Curriculum Review
rationale: Procedural regulation implementing the multi-year curriculum review cycle; no decision about an individual student.

title: Instructional Program
rationale: Directs teachers to use assessment data to inform instructional strategy generally; does not establish a placement, tracking, or grading determination process.

title: School-Sponsored Student Publications
rationale: Governs review/appeal of prohibited student expression in publications, which is not one of the enumerated consequential-decision categories.

title: Health Education
rationale: Curriculum content and parental exemption notice requirements; no scored or automated decision about a student.

title: Abbreviated School Day
rationale: IEP/504 teams place students with disabilities on abbreviated schedules, a special-education placement decision directly affecting FAPE/LRE.

title: Home-Based Education
rationale: A standardized-test percentile score is used as a formulaic cutoff that determines whether a student remains exempt from compulsory attendance or must enroll in school.

title: Elementary-Based Child Care
rationale: Vendor RFP/selection process for elementary child-care services; not a decision about any individual student's educational status.

title: Instructional Resources and Materials
rationale: Textbook/material approval delegation and standards; not a decision about a student.

title: Instructional Resources and Materials
rationale: Committee procedure for selecting and adopting instructional materials; no individualized student decision.

title: Library Materials Selection and Adoptions
rationale: Sets criteria for library media selection; no decision about an individual student.

title: Field Trips and Other Activities Away from School
rationale: Governs trip approval and disability accommodations for a single activity; an event-level accommodation, not an educational placement/tracking determination.

title: Field Trips
rationale: Administrative approval procedure for field trips; no consequential decision about a student.

title: Graduation and Diploma Requirements
rationale: Diploma issuance is gated on meeting or exceeding numeric cut scores on vendor-administered assessments, a graduation/diploma consequential decision.

title: Graduation and Diploma Requirements
rationale: Gates graduation and endorsement awards on assessment cut scores and a 3.6 GPA threshold, both formulaic consequential decisions.

title: Graduation Exercises
rationale: Ceremony logistics and neutrality rules; diploma eligibility itself is determined under IKF, not here.

title: Student Participation in Curricular Instruction, Programs and Activities
rationale: Parent-initiated, human-decided curriculum exemption/appeal process with no scoring, placement, or grading determination.

title: Flag Displays
rationale: Flag display protocol only; no decision about any person.

title: Religious Observances and Displays
rationale: Provides a categorical accommodation shielding religious absences from grade penalty but does not itself establish or operate a scored grading decision process.

title: Teaching about Religion
rationale: Applies a three-part constitutional test to curriculum content decisions; not a decision about an individual student.

title: Teaching Methods
rationale: General personalized-instruction philosophy and differentiated teaching methods; not a formal tracking, placement, or grading system.

title: Instructional Resources and Materials
rationale: Budget allocation rules for textbooks and materials; no decision about a student.

title: Supplementary Materials
rationale: Approval process for supplementary instructional materials (including assessments as a material type); not a decision about an individual student's status.

title: Grade Point Averages/Class Rankings
rationale: Formulaic GPA weighting and decile-based class-rank calculation directly determines each student's academic standing, a grading/ranking consequential decision.

title: Harassment or Discrimination of Students
rationale: Governs harassment/discrimination investigations and determinations of whether misconduct affected a student's IEP/504 FAPE eligibility (a special-education placement decision), with team convening and education-record handling.

title: School Attendance Areas
rationale: Establishes each student's neighborhood-school assignment (an enrollment/placement decision) using residence and enrollment data, with community/parent notice on boundary changes.

title: Compulsory Attendance
rationale: Includes a grade-retention placement decision honored on parent request, a covered grade/class-assignment matter with a district consultation/review step.

title: Entrance Age Requirements
rationale: Directly governs kindergarten/first-grade admission and discretionary 'highly advanced gifted' early-entrance determinations, a covered admission/placement decision about individual students.

title: Admission of Non-Immigrant Foreign Students
rationale: Governs admission eligibility and discretionary denial authority for exchange students, relying on third-party visa/agency vetting (vendor-adjacent) and a covered admission decision.

title: Homeless Students
rationale: Governs enrollment/placement eligibility determinations for homeless students, confidential handling of housing-status education records, written notice, and a formal appeal/dispute-resolution process to a state coordinator.

title: Homeless Students (Regulation)
rationale: Implements the enrollment placement determination, records-handling, notice, and appeal procedures for homeless students, mirroring the covered decision and human-review elements of Policy JFABD.

title: Choice/Open Enrollment
rationale: Directly governs student enrollment/placement through priority-ranked and lottery-based allocation, denial criteria, disability-data restrictions, an online application platform, and a superintendent appeal process.

title: Student Withdrawal From School/Dropouts
rationale: Governs formal dropout classification and reporting to the state along with structured human-review meetings (counselor, principal, parents) regarding a student's continued enrollment status.

title: Assignment of New Students to Classes and Grade Levels
rationale: Directly governs grade/class placement decisions based on standardized testing, transfer credits, and principal assessment, with parent notification and a two-tier appeal procedure.

title: Open/Closed Campus
rationale: Purely operational campus-access and visitor-management rules with no enrollment, placement, discipline, or personal-data determination about a specific student.

title: Student Government
rationale: Advisory student-council governance with principal-delegated decisions on clubs/activities/elections, not a covered consequential decision or personal-data process.

title: Student Dress
rationale: Dress-code enforcement follows a graduated notice/options process that can escalate to suspension or expulsion, a covered discipline outcome, though not itself data- or technology-driven.

title: Threat Assessments
rationale: Governs individualized student risk/safety determinations and records disclosure exceptions — the paradigmatic domain where automated risk-scoring tools raise ADMT concerns, currently handled by human/law-enforcement evaluation.

title: Bullying Prevention and Education
rationale: Governs bullying investigations and determinations of FAPE impact for IEP/504 students, including student surveys and data collection, with principal investigation and team-convening review.

title: Student Distribution of Non-School Materials
rationale: Establishes a principal approval/denial process with a defined appeal to the assistant superintendent and ties policy violations to student discipline.

title: Secret Societies/Gang Activity Dress
rationale: Governs administrator inferences about gang/secret-society involvement (with nondiscrimination limits on classification) and parent notification, leading to discretionary student discipline.

title: Student Conduct Involving Drugs and Alcohol
rationale: Sets a graduated, offense-count-based suspension and expulsion framework directly determining student discipline consequences.

title: Student Conduct Involving Weapons
rationale: Directly governs mandatory/discretionary expulsion and suspension determinations for weapons violations, including a superintendent discretion/override step.

title: Student Possession and Use of Personal Communication Devices
rationale: Governs device confiscation, academic penalties (grading), and suspension/expulsion consequences, and references documented disability/accommodation plans.

title: Searches
rationale: Establishes human reasonable-suspicion determinations for student searches with disciplinary consequences and requires documentation/handling of seized evidence.

title: Interscholastic Athletic Training and Personal Conduct Rules
rationale: Imposes tiered competition suspension and permanent ineligibility determinations based on violation counts, directly affecting student athletic eligibility.

title: Contests for Students
rationale: Governs program-level approval of contest opportunities for classes/grade levels rather than any consequential decision about an individual student or staff member.

title: Student Physicals for School Athletics
rationale: Conditions individual student athletic eligibility on a medical certification record, a low-discretion compliance gate rather than a discretionary decision process.

title: Student Discipline
rationale: Central discipline policy governing suspension, disability-related placement changes, FERPA-bound sharing of discipline data, and a mandatory manifestation-determination human-review step.

title: Physical Intervention, Restraint, Seclusion and Time-Out
rationale: Governs high-stakes restraint/seclusion decisions about individual students, requiring written parent notice, incident-report recordkeeping, and annual superintendent review.

title: Detention of Students
rationale: Authorizes an administrator's disciplinary detention decision about an individual student with a required prior parent notification.

title: Disciplinary Removal from Classroom
rationale: Governs in-school-suspension-style classroom removal decisions requiring administrator review, family notice, and behavior-plan recordkeeping.

title: Discipline of Habitually Disruptive Students
rationale: Establishes a records-based habitually-disruptive designation with written notice requirements, student-file documentation, and principal review culminating in suspension/expulsion.

title: Suspension/Expulsion of Students
rationale: Core suspension/expulsion policy weighing individual student factors (age, disciplinary history, disability status) and providing superintendent determination with board appeal.

title: Procedures Regarding Suspension/Expulsion of Students
rationale: Implements the full due-process notice, evidentiary hearing, and hearing-officer human-review procedure for suspension and expulsion decisions.

title: Grounds for Suspension/Expulsion of Students
rationale: Defines the substantive grounds/criteria used to determine suspension or expulsion, including a required human safety-threat determination for young students.

title: IMMUNIZATION OF STUDENTS
rationale: Governs individualized health-exemption determinations affecting attendance eligibility and requires retained medical/exemption documentation, warranting an ADMT inventory check and recordkeeping alignment.

title: ADMINISTERING MEDICINE TO STUDENTS/ASTHMA, ALLERGY AND ANAPHYLAXIS
rationale: Establishes case-by-case health/medical determinations (self-carry authorization, individualized Health Plans) and maintains sensitive student medical records requiring PII classification and ADMT inventory review.

title: ADMINISTERING MEDICAL MARIJUANA TO QUALIFIED STUDENTS
rationale: Governs individualized medical-eligibility determinations and confidential medical documentation for qualified students, requiring an ADMT inventory check and recordkeeping oversight.

title: MEDICALLY NECESSARY TREATMENT IN SCHOOL SETTING
rationale: Creates a formal team-based consequential health/educational-access decision process with parent notice, hearing, and appeal rights that must be reconciled with SB 26-189's meaningful human-review and override requirements.

title: AUTHORIZING PRIVATE HEALTH-CARE SPECIALISTS FOR TREATMENT
rationale: Implements JLCDC's individual treatment determinations and imposes FERPA/HIPAA confidentiality and consent/documentation obligations tied to a student's health record and treatment access.

title: ADMINISTERING MEDICINE TO STUDENTS
rationale: Sets detailed individual student medication-administration recordkeeping, retention, and error-reporting requirements constituting protected student health records.

title: SCREENING/TESTING OF STUDENTS
rationale: Directly regulates student screening/testing and psychological assessment, mandating advance notice, consent exceptions, PII protection, and parental review/objection rights central to SB 26-189's covered domain and duties.

title: STUDENT DISMISSAL PRECAUTIONS
rationale: Purely operational identity-verification procedure for early dismissal with no automated decision-making, education-record handling, or enumerated consequential-decision domain implicated.

title: STUDENT VEHICLE USE AND PARKING
rationale: Manual, non-automated parking-privilege and search procedure that does not itself make a covered consequential decision (any resulting discipline is adjudicated under separate discipline policies).

title: STUDENT FEES, FINES AND CHARGES
rationale: Administrative fee-schedule and waiver policy with no automated decision-making or personal data/technology handling; consequences are financial/administrative rather than an enumerated consequential decision.

title: STUDENT RECORDS/RELEASE OF INFORMATION ON STUDENTS
rationale: Comprehensive FERPA education-records policy governing access, amendment appeals, disclosure, and annual notice, squarely within SB 26-189's recordkeeping, notice, and human-review/correction duties.

title: PRIVACY AND PROTECTION OF CONFIDENTIAL STUDENT RECORD INFORMATION
rationale: Governs confidential student PII security/retention, holds school-service-contract vendors accountable for misuse, and provides a parent complaint/appeal process, directly matching SB 26-189's recordkeeping, vendor-binding, and human-review duties.

title: STUDENT USE OF DISTRICT INFORMATION TECHNOLOGY
rationale: Governs pervasive technology monitoring, AI tool use, student data handling, and technology-related discipline consequences, implicating ADMT inventory, PII classification, vendor tools, and human review of resulting discipline.

title: YOUTH SUICIDE PREVENTION
rationale: Mandates suicide-risk screening and mental-health assessment of students, a covered screening/health domain, with specialist human review and cross-referenced records obligations.
